summary:
The individual likely has acute hepatitis B and no prior exposure since both HBsAg and Anti-HBc are present; repeat testing for IgM anti-HBs and quantification of HBV DNA levels every 6 months can help monitor resolution or progression. Continuous surveillance helps determine whether treatment becomes necessary based on viral load dynamics and potential liver damage indicators.
The person probably has recently acquired acute Hepatitis B as evidenced by only detecting HBsAg despite lacking protective immunity from past infection marked by Absence of Anti-HBc antibodies ; regular retesting including measurement of circulating virus quantity via Hbsv dna assays at intervals such as biannually will aid tracking recovery process versus development toward Chronic condition demanding therapeutic intercession when warranted due to escalating hepatic dysfunction signals detected during monitoring phases .
